html:
Exclusive to:

This article is incomplete because of the evil sabotage by Maxwell....
Please help to improve the article, or discuss the issue with the community on the talk page according to the talk page etiquette or on our Discord.

The Magnifying Glass is a craftable Item exclusive to the Hamlet DLC. It is found in the Treasure Hunting Tab, requires an Iron Ore, a Blue Gem and Twigs to craft, and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.

The Magnifying Glass can be used to light burnable objects on fire from a distance, and to craft The Tinkerer's Tower. However, its main purpose is to investigate Peculiar Objects and Suspicious Cracks in the Ancient Pig Ruins. After using a Magnifying Glass to investigate a Peculiar Object or Suspicious Crack, the Character will state clearly whether or not the Object conceals an item or the Crack conceals a door to a hidden room. In the first case, destroying the Object (e.g. chopping down a Tree and digging up its stump or mining a Boulder) will reveal one of the following items:

  • Flint
  • Gold Nugget
  • Oinc
  • Tenpiece Oinc
  • Relic Fragment
  • Lost Totem
  • Lost Idol
  • Trinkets

Mants are afraid of the Magnifying Glass, and will panic if the player is holding one while nearby.


Usage

×2

×4

​​ Tips

  • Peculiar Objects are also found in Hamlet compatible worlds.

Trivia

  • Mants being afraid of Magnifying Glasses is a reference to the practice of using magnifying glasses to burn insects.
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

“Science awaits!”
–Wilson
“A poor substitute for my lighter.”
–Willow
“Hehe! Eye is big now!”
–Wolfgang
“For a closer look at life's horrors.”
–Wendy
“SEARCH MODE ACCESSORY”
–WX-78
“A fine tool for discovery.”
–Wickerbottom
“Pretty clever.”
–Woodie
“Let's see what we can find.”
–Maxwell
“Excellent! A burning glass!”
–Wagstaff
“It giveth me mighty eyesight.”
–Wigfrid
“Gee. We can see all kinds of things now.”
–Webber
“It's amazing what you can find with this.”
–Walani
“To see the finest details.”
–Warly
“'Tis a spyglass o' tha land.”
–Woodlegs
“IT LOOKETH 'PON UNDISCOVER'D STUFFS”
–Wilba
“Peeper”
–Wormwood
“For finding the tiny hidden treasures. And burning stuff.”
–Wheeler
The Magnifying Glass is a craftable Item exclusive to the Hamlet DLC. It is found in the Treasure Hunting Tab, requires an Iron Ore, a Blue Gem and Twigs to craft, and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.
The Magnifying Glass can be used to light burnable objects on fire from a distance, and to craft The Tinkerer's Tower. However, its main purpose is to investigate Peculiar Objects and Suspicious Cracks in the Ancient Pig Ruins. After using a Magnifying Glass to investigate a Peculiar Object or Suspicious Crack, the Character will state clearly whether or not the Object conceals an item or the Crack conceals a door to a hidden room. In the first case, destroying the Object (e.g. chopping down a Tree and digging up its stump or mining a Boulder) will reveal one of the following items:
Flint
Gold Nugget
Oinc
Tenpiece Oinc
Relic Fragment
Lost Totem
Lost Idol
Trinkets
Mants are afraid of the Magnifying Glass, and will panic if the player is holding one while nearby.

​​ Tips[edit | edit source]
Peculiar Objects are also found in Hamlet compatible worlds.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Mants being afraid of Magnifying Glasses is a reference to the practice of using magnifying glasses to burn insects.

And burning stuff. |choose = wendy}} The '''Magnifying Glass''' is a craftable [[Item]] exclusive to the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC. It is found in the [[Treasure Hunting Tab]], requires an [[Iron Ore]], a [[Blue Gem]] and [[Twigs]] to craft, and an [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype. The Magnifying Glass can be used to light burnable objects on fire from a distance, and to craft [[The Tinkerer's Tower]]. However, its main purpose is to investigate [[Peculiar Objects]] and Suspicious Cracks in the [[Ancient Pig Ruins]]. After using a Magnifying Glass to investigate a Peculiar Object or Suspicious Crack, the Character will state clearly whether or not the Object conceals an item or the Crack conceals a door to a hidden room. In the first case, destroying the Object (e.g. chopping down a [[Tree]] and digging up its stump or mining a [[Boulder]]) will reveal one of the following items:
